channel.title: AI Agents Brief
channel.description: Daily intelligence brief on AI agents, autonomous systems, and the agentic AI landscape.
channel.itunes:author: Media Verticals

channel.itunes:summary: Daily intelligence brief on AI agents, autonomous systems, and the agentic AI landscape.

item.title: AI Agents Brief: What's really happening here is the gap between agent capability demos and what...
item.description:
Welcome back. We're looking at a pretty revealing moment in agent development today—and it's honestly more interesting because it's not the story people expected.
Let's start with the most important signal, because it reframes everything else: Claude Code is hitting a serious wall. We're seeing wid...

item.title: AI Agents Brief: We're seeing multiple builders shipping production agents right now that run...
item.description:

The top signal today is this: Claude Code is becoming the actual runtime environment for deployed agents. And I want to be clear ...

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — April 1, 2026
item.description:
Hey everyone. Welcome back. We've got a lot to unpack today, and honestly, this is one of those moments where you can feel the industry actually shifting beneath your feet.
The big story is that Claude Code got leaked. We're talking 512,000 lines of TypeScript across more than 2,000 files just ende...
item.itunes:author: Media Verticals
item.author: mediaverticals@proton.me
item.dc:creator: Media Verticals

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 30, 2026
item.description: Hey, welcome back. Today we're looking at what happens when you take a coding tool and builders start treating it like the operating system for autonomous work. And there's a really interesting pattern emerging — Claude Code has kind of accidentally become the runtime that everything else is buildin...
item.itunes:author: Media Verticals

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 29, 2026
item.description:

So the biggest thing I'm noticing today is that we're hitting a wall with the idea of general-purpose coding ...

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 28, 2026
item.description:
Welcome back. Today's looking at a critical inflection point in how we're actually building and deploying AI agents in production. And the headline is pretty straightforward: the industry is shipping agents faster than it's securing them, and that gap is closing in ways that matter.
Let's start wit...
item.itunes:author: Media Verticals

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 25, 2026
item.description:

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 24, 2026
item.description:
Welcome. Today we're looking at a meaningful shift in how agents are actually being deployed in production, and it centers on a single insight: agents now need to verify their own work before anything ships.
Let's start with the headline development. There's a tool called ProofShot that just hit 11...
item.itunes:author: Media Verticals
item.author: mediaverticals@proton.me
item.dc:creator: Media Verticals

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 23, 2026
item.description:
Hey, welcome back. We're looking at a pretty significant inflection point in how AI agents are actually being used—and I want to start with what I think is the most important signal of the week.
Agent teams are shipping in production right now. Not as experiments, not as proofs of concept—actual pr...

item.description:

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 20, 2026
item.description:

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 19, 2026
item.description:
So we're watching agents move from experimental to infrastructure, and that shift is creating some really specific problems that are worth your attention. Let me walk through what's happening.
The headline today is that Claude Code just shipped something called context_fork that genuinely solves on...

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 18, 2026
item.description:

item.title: ai_agents Daily Brief — March 17, 2026
item.description: Welcome back. The kind of shift that doesn't feel dramatic when it's happening but will loo...
